rulings.Case Title: RAY BRONNER VS. RICHARD S KAIHARA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order That The Truth Of Any Matters In Request For Admissions Are Deemed Admittedthat Pltf Serve Responses To Interrogatories And Document Request Discovery With Objections Waived And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 15, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANTS RICHARD KAIHARA, JEFF LANCE, HAYMAN BROTHERS, INC.'S Motion For Order That The Truth Of Any Matters In Request For Admissions Are Deemed Admitted that Plaintiff Serve Responses To Interrogatories And Document Request Discovery With Objections Waived And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The matter is off calendar. Pursuant to San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules of Court, Rule 2.6B, a file-endorsed courtesy copy of any brief, memorandum, motion or response thereto must be lodged with the clerk of the Discovery Department prior to the hearing date. The Court did not receive a courtesy copy of the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. MIKE O'REILLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike; Memo Of P&A In Support Of
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 15, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANTS DAN NOLTING and PINE GROVE GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Strike. Granted in part. The motion to strike is granted as to "1. Fraud [] 2. Harassment" in the caption, and "1. For damages to plaintiff's property that was incurred by defendants failure to perform in a sum according to proof" in the first paragraph of the prayer. There are no causes of action for fraud and harassment in the complaint, and these phrases are not supported by allegations in the complaint. The motion to strike as to the second and third paragraphs of the prayer are granted. The balance of the motion to strike is denied as moot in light of the Court's ruling on the demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. MIKE O'REILLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 15, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANTS DAN NOLTING and PINE GROVE GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained as to Defendant Dan Nolting, with twenty days leave to amend. The Complaint is uncertain in that it fails to identify to which defendant or defendants each cause of action is directed. CCP 430.10(f) and Rule of Court 2.112 (4). As such, Defendant cannot reasonably respond. In addition, the demurrer is sustained, with twenty days leave to amend on the following additional grounds: As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege what property was taken and what force or fear was used to take it. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege an economic relationship with a third party. As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege outrageous conduct and severe emotional distress. As to the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts to support this cause of action. The demurrer of Defendant Pine Grove Group, Inc. is off calendar because Pine Grove Group, Inc. has been dismissed with prejudice from this action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DUNG TUYEN VS. PAUL HEMMING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Comple And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 15, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANTS PAUL HEMMING, ZEN COMPOUND, LLC DBA TEMPLE NIGHTCLUB'S Notice Of Motion To Compel And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant ZEN COMPOUND, LLC dba TEMPLE NIGHTCLUB's Motion to Compel is GRANTED. On or before December 14, 2012, Plaintiff DUNG TUYEN shall provide, without objection: (1) a Statement of Damages pursuant to C.C.P. section 425.11; (2) all documents responsive to Defendant's Request for Production, Set One, and a verification therefor; (3) substantive supplemental responses to Defendant's Form Interrogatories, Set One, and a verification therefor; and (4) substantive supplemental responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One, and a verification therefor. Defendant to prepare an Order. Defendant's Request for Sanctions is DENIED for lack of proof. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 15, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANTS GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC. and GOOGLE INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued on the court's own motion to December 11, 2012 due to the recusal of Judge Miller. The Court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. The reply papers indicate that an opposition was filed and served. The Court's register of action does not reflect that an opposition was ever filed, however. In addition, the Court does not have a courtesy copy of any opposing papers. The matter is continued to allow Plaintiff the opportunity to file the opposition and provide a courtesy copy in compliance with 2.6B. Plaintiff shall provide a cover letter with its courtesy copy reflecting the new hearing date to Department 302, no later than November 16, 2012 at 4:00 pm, or the demurrer will be sustained. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN CHAN VS. DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Or Dimsiss Litigation By Defts
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 15, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Or Dimsiss Litigation By Defendants. Denied, without prejudice. The petition itself is not verified, and Delta Dental of California has not presented any admissible evidence in support of the petition. Further, the purported 2006 contract is not signed by any of the parties. (Petition, Exhibit B.) Delta Dental of California's introduction of evidence in connection with its reply brief is improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
